sourceRef.citation: Kourovka Notebook, Problem 21.128 (Khukhro & Mazurov eds., ed.21)

statement.latex: Two groups$G_1$and$G_2$are said to be if there exist finite-index subgroups$H_1 G_1$and$H_2 G_2$(not necessarily of the same index) such that$H_1 H_2$. Let$A[F_4]$and$A[H_4]$denote the Artin groups of spherical types$F_4$and$H_4$, respectively. Are these two groups commensurable? This is the most difficult case in the classification of Artin groups of spherical type up to commensurability.

reiAssessment.solveRationale: Group theory IV_PROBLEM_UNDEFINED; Rei not specialized in group theory (yet)
reiAssessment.formalizationNotes: Kourovka problems are typically research-level group theory
